summary: Florida RON Compliance Phase 1 (M1) — full backend + frontend testing complete. Backend pytest 16/16 passing (state profile public/admin, FL notary onboard with validation/idempotency/duplicate detection, admin pending/verified lists, decision approve+reject, public directory sensitive-field filtering, eligibility checks). Frontend /florida landing page renders with both CTAs and FAQ; /notary/onboard/florida 5-step wizard works end-to-end (commission → bond → training → seal → review → submit), submission flips to fl-onboard-pending screen, and a verified notary (notarytest@test.com / GG987654) sees the fl-onboard-verified screen with full commission details. Only minor console warnings observed — no blockers.

ui_bugs.issue: Console warning: `value` prop on `input` should not be null. Caused by null fields (fl_seal_image_url, fl_e_signature_id, fl_training_certificate_url, notes) returned from GET /api/fl/notary/credentials being spread directly into form state. Functional but noisy.

issues: Stats card 'Verified FL notaries' initially shows '…' while count loads — could use a smaller skeleton instead of large placeholder for snappier perceived perf (cosmetic).

action_items: Optional: In FloridaNotaryOnboard.jsx, coerce null/undefined credential fields to empty strings when seeding form state (e.g., `setForm(f => ({ ...f, ...Object.fromEntries(Object.entries(d.credentials).map(([k,v])=>[k, v ?? ''])) }))`) to silence React 'value should not be null' warnings.
Optional: Add a loading skeleton for the 'Verified FL notaries' stat so it doesn't show a large '…' placeholder.
critical_code_review_comments: fl_compliance_routes.py is clean (366 lines, single responsibility, async, env-driven, excludes _id, proper 4xx/5xx codes).
Pydantic validator `_check_iso` swallows ValueError correctly; expired dates are caught in the route handler before persistence.
Public directory endpoint correctly limits fields — no bond_number, no certificate URL, no email leak.
Idempotent upsert pattern is correct: pending submissions update; verified records reject re-submit with 400.
Indexes are created on startup (user_id unique, fl_commission_number unique, state_code unique) — verified via test_onboard_rejects_duplicate_commission_by_different_user.
Minor: FL_COMMISSION_RX is defined but never enforced (only min-length check is applied). Could either remove the dead regex or enforce it for stricter format validation.

success_rate.frontend: 95% — wizard, verified state, landing all working; unauth state code-verified (browser session persistence prevented direct assert)

test_credentials: Admin admin@notarychain.com/Admin123!, Notary notarytest@test.com/Test123! (already verified with commission GG987654), Demo demo@test.com/Demo123! (used by tests; ends in 'rejected' state after the approve→reject workflow test cleanup).
seed_data_creation: Backend tests create transient FL notary credential rows under demo@test.com (rejected at end) and a TEST-RONSP-* filing record under state_compliance_profiles. No production data modified. UI wizard test created a pending submission for demo user during the e2e flow (commission ZZ999988, bond SB-UI-1).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend M1 surface is fully covered by /app/backend/tests/test_fl_compliance.py — reuse for regression when M2 (KBA) or M3 (ceremony pipeline) lands. demo@test.com FL credential is currently in 'rejected' status after this iteration; re-onboarding will work (status will return to pending_review). notarytest@test.com is verified with commission GG987654 / $25,000 bond / FloridaNotary.com training. RONSP filing TEST-RONSP-* exists in state_compliance_profiles (live_in_state=true) — when M2 testing begins, may need to reset to false if testing pre-launch state.
rca_of_issue: No bugs found requiring RCA. All assertions in the review request matched implementation behavior.
